what_it_does: Defines the ConceptV icon library as a global window.CV_ICONS object, mapping icon names to SVG path strings and exposing two browser helpers that render those paths into an SVG element or an inline SVG string.
observations: Contains roughly 100 hand-coded SVG path entries grouped by thematic category comments.
Each icon targets a 24x24 viewBox with a 1.5 px stroke, rounded caps/joins, and currentColor tinting.
Most paths are stroke-only, but some entries intentionally use fill=currentColor, stroke=none, or embedded text elements.
Provides window.CV_ICONS.svg() to create an SVGSVGElement and window.CV_ICONS.markup() to return an SVG string.
Header documents intended React usage via a CvIcon component and a build script scripts/build-icon-svgs.html that is not present in the file.
Uses global namespace assignment rather than ES module exports.
standouts: Claims to be the single source of truth for the ConceptV icon set.
Includes domain-specific icons such as crane, floorplan, building-dollar, m2, vr-headset, drone, and blueprint.
Mixes generic UI icons with architecture/real-estate themed glyphs.
Hardcodes font-family DM Sans inside text-bearing icons.
conventions: 1.5 px stroke width applied by the renderer.
stroke-linecap and stroke-linejoin set to round.
currentColor used for stroke to allow context tinting.
Icon keys use lowercase hyphenated names.
concerns: Pollutes the global window namespace instead of exporting a module.
Uses innerHTML to inject SVG path strings.
Embedded text relies on DM Sans being loaded, which is not guaranteed.
Some icons deviate from the documented no-fill rule.
No TypeScript types or runtime schema for the icon catalog.
No visible mechanism to keep generated per-icon SVG files in sync with this source file.
notes: This is the browser-side icon registry and renderer for the ConceptV design system, holding the canonical path data and two convenience renderers.
questions: Where is the CvIcon React component referenced in the header?
What does scripts/build-icon-svgs.html produce and how is it kept in sync?
Why is the library attached to window rather than exported as a module?
Is DM Sans guaranteed to be loaded wherever text-bearing icons render?
How are icon additions versioned or validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the single source of truth for ConceptV icons and to provide rendering helpers; the file does provide the icon dictionary and helpers, though it also points to external build tooling and a React wrapper that are not shown here.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a centralized, reusable, themeable icon library with a simple name-based API for both React and raw HTML, plus a build pipeline that emits standalone SVG files.
novelty: A large custom line-art icon set blending generic UI glyphs with architecture, construction, and spatial-computing motifs, stored as inline path strings with a lightweight runtime renderer.
contribution: A self-contained icon dictionary and two lightweight render helpers that let consuming code reference icons by name and produce consistent 24x24 currentColor SVG output; the categorical path definitions are the keepable asset.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-side JavaScript file that registers the ConceptV icon library as window.CV_ICONS.data with roughly 100 SVG path strings and exposes svg() and markup() helpers to render 24x24 currentColor icons.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single source of truth for ConceptV icons and provide a simple, color-agnostic rendering API for React and raw HTML consumers.
